question: What is the documented replacement for the deprecated `camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.force-fetch-all-variables` property?

a.text: The deprecation table lists `camunda.client.worker.defaults.force-fetch-all-variables` (env `CAMUNDA_CLIENT_WORKER_DEFAULTS_FORCEFETCHALLVARIABLES`) as the direct replacement.

b.text: `worker.override` is keyed by job type for per-worker overrides; it is not where you set the default for every worker.

c.text: The entire `camunda.client.zeebe` namespace is deprecated; worker defaults now sit under `camunda.client.worker.defaults`.

d.text: There is no top-level `camunda.client.force-fetch-all-variables` property; the setting is scoped to worker defaults, not the client as a whole.

explanation: When migrating from the legacy `camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.*` namespace, each leaf property keeps its name and moves under `camunda.client.worker.defaults.*`. `force-fetch-all-variables` overrides `fetch-variables` so the worker pulls every process variable on activation.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
